title: thlaspidea arvensis
intro: Thlaspidea arvensis, also known as Field Penny Cress, is a perennial plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits associated with Thlaspidea arvensis include its ability to reduce fever, relieve coughs, and soothe respiratory problems, as well as its potential to act as a diuretic. Herbal preparations of Thlaspidea arvensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, tonics, and other medicinal products.
